rulings.Case Title: 121 9TH STREET/788 MINNA STREET OWNERS' VS. 121 NINTH, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Liam Moloney Painting, Inc. And Lmp Coatings, Inc. Motion For Good Faith Settlement (Civil Code Section 877.6)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 1, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT LMP COATINGS, INC., LIAM MOLONEY PAINTING, INC.'s, Motion For Good Faith Settlement (Civil Code Section 877.6). Off calendar. All real property-related law and motion matters are to be calendared in Department 501 under SFLR 8.10A. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Defendant 426 Fillmore Association?S Motion For Court Order Reopening Discovery In Conformance With The Trial Date Of March 16, 2026, Or, Alternatively, Confirming Discovery And Motion Deadlines As Tracking The Trial Date, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 1, 2025, Line 2, DEFENDANT 426 FILLMORE ASSOCATION'S Motion For Court Order Reopening Discovery In Conformance With The Trial Date Of March 16, 2026, Or, Alternatively, Confirming Discovery And Motion Deadlines As Tracking The Trial Date, Etc. (Complete tentative ruling e-mailed to the parties). Defendant 426 Fillmore Association's Motion For Court Order Reopening Discovery In Conformance With The Trial Date Of March 16, 2026, Or, Alternatively, Confirming Discovery And Motion Deadlines As Tracking The Trial Date, Etc., is GRANTED. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MACGREGOR ET AL VS. SUNSHINE TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 1, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MACGREGOR, CAREY SUCKOW's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT. 2 - Plaintiffs' motion to strike Defendant Street Factory Media, Inc.'s answer is DENIED. Plaintiffs assert the same unpersuasive arguments as their motion to strike Defendants Forward Air, Inc., Krisnil Prasad, and Sunshine Transportation's answer. The motion is denied for the reasons set out in the Forward Air order of this day. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).
